cards.title: Components of Premium
cards.content:
ΓÇó Pure Premium: The basic mathematical cost of the risk (Losses / Total Exposure).
ΓÇó Loading: Extra charges added for administration, commissions, and profit margins.
ΓÇó Gross Premium: Pure Premium + Loadings.

cards.title: Tariff vs Non-Tariff
cards.content:
ΓÇó Tariff: Rates fixed centrally by an authority (Mostly abolished in India, except Motor Third Party).
ΓÇó Non-Tariff: Insurers are free to calculate and fix their own premium rates based on risk.

items.text: Average Clause: Applied when property is UNDER-insured. Claim is reduced in proportion to the under-insurance.
When a risk is too large, multiple insurers share the premium and the losses collectively.
Short Period Scales: Used when a policy is taken for less than 12 months. Premium is charged at a higher rate than pro-rata.

sections.title: ≡ƒÆí Exam Master Tips
items: Underwriting is the process of deciding whether to accept a risk and at what premium rate.
Minimum Premium: A base amount charged to cover administrative costs, even if the risk is very small.
Physical Hazard Inspection: A risk inspector visits factories before underwriting to check safety measures.
Deductible: The first portion of the claim that the insured must bear. It eliminates small claims.
Average Clause formula: (Sum Insured / Actual Value) * Loss Amount.
